pool.name: Smokey the Pear
pool.creator: Glenn Strycker
pool.method: Shake hard with ice.  Strain into a chilled martini glass.
pool.tagline: Smokey Mezcal cocktail with pear juice and rhubarb bitters
pool.profile: Smoky-meets-pear shaken cocktail. Desert/Mexican thematic -- the prickly pear preserves are the structural pun (a "pear" that isn't a pear, growing in mezcal country). Recommended sub-spirit pairing: white Jamaican rum first choice (funky/vegetal echoes agave's roasted notes; Jamaican esters complement pear esters), gin second (London Dry > floral botanicals can complement pear, but heavy florals will fight it), vodka third (subtracts smoke without adding character).
pool.why_it_works: Mezcal offers smokey flavors that compliment the pear

ratings.notes: Dial back the mezcal and increase the pear flavor

pool.name: Amaretto Sour, Reimagined
pool.tagline: The Army & Navy's almond-loving cousin — richer, simpler, and always in the cabinet.

ingredients.name: London Dry Gin
ingredients.notes: A juniper-forward gin keeps the drink bracing against the sweet liqueur.

ingredients.name: Amaretto
ingredients.notes: Disaronno or Lazzaroni — provides the almond note that orgeat would, plus a touch of sweetness and depth.

ingredients.name: Rich Simple Syrup (2:1)
ingredients.notes: Optional — only if your amaretto is on the drier side or you want to round the edges.

pool.method: Add all ingredients to a shaker with ice. Shake hard for 10–12 seconds until well chilled and frothy. Double-strain into a chilled coupe.
pool.garnish: Lemon twist expressed over the surface, draped on the rim; brandied cherry dropped in.
pool.tasting_notes: Bright lemon and juniper up front, with a warm marzipan-and-stone-fruit midpalate from the amaretto. Finishes clean and slightly bitter from the gin botanicals — drier than a classic Amaretto Sour, sharper than an Army & Navy.
pool.why_it_works: This is essentially a gin-based Amaretto Sour, and it shares DNA with the classic Army & Navy: spirit + lemon + nutty sweetener. Amaretto subs beautifully for orgeat at roughly the same volume because both deliver almond character, though amaretto brings alcohol and apricot-pit complexity that orgeat lacks. If you ever want to go further afield, Frangelico (hazelnut) or Nocino (green walnut) work on the same template. Pantry-friendly, no specialty syrup required.
pool.source_prompt: Tweak of "Army & Navy": Find a good substitution for Orgeat, as some people don't like it and I don't always have it on hand.  Use something more interesting than simple syrup, though... are there any standard classic cocktails like Army & Navy except use a sweet nutty liqueur like Amaretto?
